comments.body: Changes LGTM, travis isn't hip though.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 389d49b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@vrothberg Is this something to do with a mismatch of tools?  I don't see any issues with dirty git, but it is seeing it on Ubuntu?

> @vrothberg Is this something to do with a mismatch of tools? I don't see any issues with dirty git, but it is seeing it on Ubuntu?

Are you on F31 and go 1.13? We can't use go 1.13 for vendoring unless _the entire CI_ uses it as well. Go 1.13 removes some files that previous versions don't. It's okay to the remove them but it's annoying for our ./vendor enforcement.

What you can do is run a `golang:1.12` container and do a `go mod vendor` or `make vendor` in the mounted project. That's what I am doing at the moment.

With F31 waiting just around the corner, we need to find a solution for this issue. However, we can't assume all developers/contributors are on go 1.13, so I suggest to add another make target `make vendor-in-container` which runs all the magic in a `golang:1.13` container, so we provide an easy workaround.
